description: Add latency budget definition module and budget assertion helpers, then create latency budget integration test, memory stability test, cost budget test, and CI budget check script for Phase 41 hard gates.
steps: Create packages/quant-live/src/budget/latency-budget.ts with: (1) LATENCY_BUDGETS constant defining broadband and mobile_4g profiles with numeric ms thresholds for asr_partial (200/400), llm_first_token (350/700), tts_first_audio (500/1000), total_first_response (500/1000). (2) Export types LatencyProfile ('broadband' | 'mobile_4g'), BudgetStage, BudgetResult. (3) Function checkBudget(metrics: Record<BudgetStage, number>, profile: LatencyProfile) => { passed: boolean; violations: { stage: string; budget: number; actual: number; overBy: number }[] }.

Create packages/quant-live/src/budget/budget-assertions.ts with: (1) assertBudget(metrics, profile, tolerance?: number) that throws with a clear message if any stage exceeds budget * (1 + tolerance). Default tolerance 0.10 (10%). (2) formatViolation helper that produces CI-friendly error messages showing stage, expected, actual, overage. (3) BudgetReport type and generateReport function that returns structured JSON output.

Create packages/quant-live/src/__tests__/latency-budget.test.ts with: (1) Tests for checkBudget and assertBudget directly with known inputs. (2) Full simulated pipeline test: create a LivePipeline with mock ASR provider (delays ~100-180ms), mock LLM provider (first token ~150-300ms using async generators with setTimeout delays), mock TTS provider (first chunk ~80-150ms). Run full pipeline and assert total latency under broadband and 4g budgets. Use vi.useFakeTimers() to make timing deterministic where possible or measure actual real delays with tolerance.

Create packages/quant-live/src/__tests__/memory-stability.test.ts with: (1) Test that runs 100+ conversation turns in a loop (feed audio -> ASR result -> LLM stream -> TTS synthesis). (2) After each batch of turns, call process.memoryUsage().heapUsed. (3) Assert that heap growth from turn 10 to turn 100+ is less than 5MB (no leak). (4) Verify event listener count does not grow. (5) Use mock providers with minimal overhead to keep test fast.

Create packages/quant-live/src/__tests__/cost-budget.test.ts with: (1) Define cost constants: LLM cost per 1K tokens (input/output), ASR cost per minute, TTS cost per 1K chars. Use realistic estimates (e.g., GPT-4o-mini-like pricing). (2) Simulate 1 minute of conversation: ~150 words spoken by user (ASR), ~200 tokens LLM output, ~800 chars TTS. (3) Calculate total cost and assert it is below $0.005/min hard gate.

Create packages/quant-live/src/budget/run-budget-check.ts with: (1) Main function that imports checkBudget, runs budget validation against sample metrics. (2) Outputs JSON results to stdout. (3) Exits with code 1 if any budget violated. (4) Can be run via ts-node or vitest as a script.

Export the new budget module from packages/quant-live/src/index.ts: export { checkBudget, LATENCY_BUDGETS } from './budget/latency-budget.js'; export { assertBudget, generateReport } from './budget/budget-assertions.js';
acceptance_criteria: checkBudget returns passed: true when all metrics are within budget thresholds
checkBudget returns passed: false with correct violations when metrics exceed budgets
assertBudget does not throw when metrics are within budget + tolerance
assertBudget throws with clear error message when metrics exceed budget + tolerance
Simulated pipeline test passes asserting broadband budget (sub-500ms total)
Simulated pipeline test passes asserting 4G budget (sub-1000ms total)
Memory stability test runs 100+ turns and confirms heap growth < 5MB
Cost budget test calculates cost/min and asserts < $0.005
run-budget-check.ts exits 0 on passing budgets and exits 1 on violation
All existing 179 tests continue to pass
Total new code is within 500 LOC
verification: pnpm --filter @quant/quant-live test -- --run (all tests pass including new ones)
findings: All 196 tests pass (179 existing + 17 new). Typecheck passes. LOC total is 450 (within 500 limit). Prettier ran via lint-staged pre-commit hook without issues.
